name: Orenburg Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the courage of the people and the historic Cossack banners of the Orenburg frontier

description: A red field charged in the centre with the full coat of arms of Orenburg Oblast, occupying about three-fifths of the flag's height.
history: Adopted on 29 October 1997 by the Legislative Assembly of Orenburg Oblast; the central emblem reproduces the historic 1782 arms of the Orenburg Governorate.

features.symbolism: red is the traditional colour of the Cossack banners and a symbol of the courage of the Orenburg frontier.

features.role: coat of arms of Orenburg Oblast
features.symbolism: the full coat of arms of Orenburg Oblast: a quartered shield bearing a Saint Andrew's cross, a marten and a Russian imperial eagle, ensigned by the imperial crown and surrounded by an oak wreath bound with the Saint Andrew's ribbon.
